title: identity — Eloquent observers

handles: creating → normalise email via CITEXT (Postgres does this at DB level; the observer additionally lowercases + trims for consistency)
creating → refuse when email already exists (UNIQUE constraint would catch it too — the observer surfaces a clean `IDENTITY_EMAIL_TAKEN` error before the DB round-trip)
creating → gate on CompliantAgePolicy — refuse when dob resolves the applicant below the effective compliance floor (COPPA / GDPR per-country)
creating → initialise password_history_hashed to [] (empty JSONB array) so the rolling-window check has a starting point
creating → stamp password_changed_at with now() so the age-based rotation policy has a baseline

updating (email dirty) → refuse when the new email exists on another Identity; NULL email_verified_at + email_verification_token_hash to force re-verification; issue a fresh EmailVerificationToken via auth::EmailVerificationTokenIssuer

updating (password_hash dirty) → push the OLD hash onto password_history_hashed (trim to config('identity.password.history_size')); stamp password_changed_at; fire PasswordChanged with the resolved trigger
updating (mfa_secret_encrypted transition NULL → non-null) → refuse when no matching TOTP confirmation code proved possession in the same request; fire MfaEnrolled
updating (mfa_secret_encrypted transition non-null → NULL) → purge mfa_recovery_codes_hashed in the same transaction; fire MfaDisabled
updating (locked_until transition NULL → non-null) → fire IdentityLocked with computed lockout_number
updating (locked_until transition non-null → NULL) → fire IdentityUnlocked with trigger resolved from the update context (auto_expiry vs admin_action)
updating (email_verified_at transition NULL → non-null) → fire IdentityEmailVerified
deleting (soft) → emit `stackra.identity.erasure.soft_delete` metric; fire IdentityErased(erasure_phase='soft_delete'); write compliance-audit entry with the actor + reason
restoring → refuse when hold-period has elapsed (must-be-hard-deleted by then); clear deleted_at; write audit entry
forceDeleted → fire IdentityErased(erasure_phase='hard_delete') AFTER the row is actually gone (via afterCommit)

notes: The observer is registered by IdentityServiceProvider::boot(). It runs BEFORE HasActivityLog + HasAudit hooks so audit trails see the fully-normalised row.
Every `refuse` case throws a subclass of `Stackra\Identity\Exceptions\IdentityViolationException` — mapped to the errors in `errors.json`.
The `updating (password_hash dirty)` branch is idempotent: pushing the same hash twice within the same transaction is a no-op (the hash lookup dedupes).
